summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that a malicious pickle payload in the version cache file executes arbitrary code when the outdated module loads the cache.
expected_security_impact: The malicious pickle payload should execute during cache deserialization, creating a proof file that demonstrates arbitrary code execution.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh successfully built the Docker image, created a malicious pickle payload with a __reduce__ method that executes a shell command, and confirmed that the payload executed during cache loading, creating the proof file with the expected marker.
verification_method: The verify script creates a malicious pickle object with a __reduce__ method that executes a shell command writing a unique marker to a file. After the outdated module loads the cache via pickle.load(), it checks for the proof file and marker to confirm code execution.
evidence_produced_by_run: Malicious pickle payload was created with __reduce__ method for code execution
The payload was written to the expected cache file location
The outdated module loaded the cache file during initialization
The proof file was created at /tmp/evohunt_poc_proof.txt
The unique marker was found in the proof file, confirming arbitrary code execution

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh mounts the target repository from /Users/mac/ResearchSpace/opencode/EvoHunt-DryRun/advisory_4c338aa8/repo as read-only into the container at /target-repo.
